close_reason: Added Drop impl that closes session and conditionally calls C_Finalize
description:
Trigger: Pkcs11Backend is dropped while the process keeps running (e.g. a long-lived signing service rotates which token it uses).

Maintenance consequence: PKCS#11 spec requires C_Finalize to release library resources. cryptoki::context::Pkcs11 may or may not call C_Finalize on Drop — verify by reading cryptoki source. For HSMs that limit concurrent open sessions per host (some network-backed PKCS#11 proxies do), failing to finalize means the slot stays 'in use' from the HSM's perspective until the calling process exits.

Combined with finding crate-secretsx-nde2.1 (multiple from_uri calls cause C_Initialize collision), the lifecycle here needs explicit thinking. Either:
(a) confirm cryptoki::Pkcs11's Drop calls C_Finalize, document this as the lifecycle guarantee, and move on; or
(b) cache Pkcs11 contexts by lib path globally with refcount semantics, calling C_Finalize when the last reference drops.

Action: read ~/GIT/cryptoki or shallow-clone parallaxsecond/rust-cryptoki to verify Pkcs11 Drop behavior, then either file a confirmation note or implement (b).
title: Pkcs11Backend doesn't impl Drop to call C_Finalize; multi-process HSM clients can leak slots
